title: Dichasianthus humilis
taxonomy: Dichasianthus humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This plant is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Dichasianthus humilis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Magnoliales, and family Annonaceae. The genus Dichasianthus is part of the family Annonaceae, and the species humilis is a specific member of this genus. This classification helps identify the plant's unique characteristics and its relationship to other species within the Annonaceae family.
